channel.title: Subcurrent | Astoria Tech Meetup
channel.description: A content aggregator for the Astoria Tech Meetup community

item.description: Ghost train is best train.

item.description: Train cinema forever. Every genre. What a romp.

item.title: The Cassandra Crossing, 1976
item.description: What a fun slow burn 70s disaster movie. The cast is insane, and I found it so compelling throughout.

item.description: This movie whips.

item.title: Hellzapoppin', 1941
item.description: This movie is so cool and legit funny. It’s so much more absurdist than most comedies, much less comedies from the fourties’

item.description: This movie is so much fun.

item.description: Never seen it before. Dumb fun. I'm in the "Swayze is charismatic" camp.

item.title: Favorite Game: Hyperbeat
item.description: Every once in a while you play a game that is unlike any other, that transcends genres, that you can feel the author speaking through it to you, and that you can tell will stick around in your mind in some way or another probably forever. Hyperbeat is one of …

item.description: Render preview environments require Blueprints, but a GitHub issue suggested a Terraform + GitHub Actions workaround. I built a reusable module for it.

item.title: 2025 retrospective with shirts
item.description:
This year I bought more shirts than ever. Mostly because I decided to go to as many concerts as I possibly could.

With the year coming to a close, I thought this would be a good moment to look back at the shirts I collected throughout the year and see …

item.title: Song of the Month: Andy Williams - Do You Hear What I Hear?
item.description:
Slightly late for November, but better late than never!

As November rolled by, and the Christmas mood took over, I put on an old school Christmas songs playlist on YouTube.

Many of the songs I was hearing for the first time. A few of my favorites were Carol of the …

item.title: Song of the Month: Christopher Cross - Sailing
item.description: This month I went on a two week long road trip through California with a friend. We sailed on Lake Tahoe, walked over sand dunes in Death Valley, and hiked between the mountains in Yosemite Valley. We saw the oldest trees on Earth at the Ancient Bristlecone Pine Forest, and …

item.title: Song of the Month: Moin - Lift You
item.description: My pick for song of the month this time around is a track that played at Spectacle Theater before the movie started (the movie was Mirage Men (2013), by the way). The lyrics are very poetic, and the instrumentation has this eerie and underground feel to it. It's a great …

item.title: Pin collection update (128 pins in three images and a table)
item.description:
It's been almost two years since I posted the "Collecting pins" post! It's time I shared an update.

Somewhere along the way I decided to get more organized and started a spreadsheet to track all the details about my pin collection (has this crossed the line to the unhealthy hobby …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: Song of the Month: Soundgarden - Black Hole Sun
item.description: Just two days ago I went to an immersive theatrical experience called Viola's Room. It started in a girl's bedroom with dim lighting and a few beds made on the floor as in a sleepover. On the headphones, a beautiful song started playing. The moment was so magical it's hard …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: Song of the Month: Junko Yagami - Bay City
item.description:
Today I want to try starting something new. Every month I'll share one song that stood out to me throughout the month.

For this first one, even though it's already midway through August, I still really feel the need to share this song that has marked my days throughout July …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: A new theme for the blog (now with 0% JavaScript!)
item.description:
I've recently updated the blog's theme and I felt I should make a post not only to document the reason but also to archive pictures of the blog before and after the change for the future.

When I created this blog, I chose nikhil-theme as the theme because I found …

item.title: Gall's law
item.description: "A complex system that works is invariably found to have evolved from a simple system that worked."

item.title: Subcurrent
item.description: Subcurrent brings together content from Astoria Tech community members into a single web page and RSS feed.

item.title: Using stow to manage dotfiles on multiple machines
item.description: If you work across multiple systems, you've likely accumulated a collection of configuration files over time.

item.title: In search of the perfect pin necklace
item.description:
Last year, on the "Collecting pins" post, I mentioned that I usually wear my pins on a beanie, but was still looking for a good alternative for the warmer months.

After thinking more about it, I realized a necklace would be the way to go. It doesn't depend on any …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: Zero to Deploy with Claude.ai and Val Town
item.description: Today I streamed my first video on Twitch, showing how to use Anthropic's Claude.ai and Val Town to ship a small frontend web app.
item.author: Ash Ryan Arnwine

item.title: CardOS: Now compiling without Arduino!
item.description: In the "CardOS: Writing an OS for the Cardputer" post I shared about the OS that I'm writing for the Cardputer and that the next step was to move away from the Arduino toolchain. It took me two months but I finally did it. The end product was this commit …

item.title: You're going to Val Town
item.description: Val Town's runnable vals (their word for "gists") can be triggered manually, via email, or as an HTTP endpoint or cron job.

item.description: A look at a Node.js library called EmbedJS for RAG AI applications along with a repo to help you get started.

item.title: The 3 Cs of Developer Advocacy
item.description: A hiring framework for Developer Advocates focused on coding ability, community stewardship, and content creation.

item.title: CardOS: Writing an OS for the Cardputer
item.description: I recently got the M5Stack's Cardputer. I was motivated to get it because I knew other people personally that had it too, so we would be able to share our progress, but I was worried it might end up being just another board that sits in my closet forever untouched …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: Binary Search in Elixir
item.description: Let's implement a quick and dirty version of binary search, and we'll improve upon it after identifying the base case and the recursive case.
item.author: David Viramontes

item.title: vCard + RSS as an alternative to social media
item.description:
Last year after talking for a while with someone during a conference they asked me for my LinkedIn to be able to connect with me, to which I answered I didn't have one.

It was many years ago when I decided to leave social media. I don't miss it. Instant …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: DevRel and AI: a department lead’s self-evaluation one year in
item.description: Reflecting on the past year's journey of integrating AI into our Developer Relations initiatives at Nylas, I'm taking a moment for self-assessment.
item.author: Ash Ryan Arnwine

item.title: Collecting pins
item.description:
It started innocently about one year ago as I was looking for a gift for a friend. We played a lot of League of Legends and Ragnarok Online back in the day, so I wanted to give him something to remember that. Eventually I came across these pins:

  • Poring/Poporing …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: Using Nylas and OpenAI to triage email
item.description: Solving email inbox triage with Nylas APIs and OpenAI GPT APIs.
item.author: Ash Ryan Arnwine

item.title: Experimenting with AI in DevRel
item.description: Using AI tools like ChatGPT and GitHub Copilot to enhance DevRel work, build inspiring code samples, and enhance developer experience.
item.author: Ash Ryan Arnwine

item.title: Create an entity resource file for OAS Tools with Prisma
item.description: Make an entity resource file with Prisma that can be used with OAS Tools to generate an Open API document and a Node.js/Express server.
item.author: Ash Ryan Arnwine

item.title: Meeting the Linux community at my first ELC
item.description:
This month I attended the Embedded Linux Conference Europe, which was co-located with the Open Source Summit Europe, and took place in Dublin, Ireland. This was my first time attending a Linux conference in-person as a contributor and it was a very special experience.

This wasn't my first ever in-person …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: Moving the blog to Codeberg
item.description:
As someone who cares about FOSS, I'm always happy to move to a FOSS alternative when one shows up, provided there aren't any big drawbacks.

Back when I was in University and starting to learn the ways of Git, I only knew about two Git hosting options: GitHub, the mainstream …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: Mac setup guide for web development
item.description: An opinionated setup guide for web development on a mac
item.author: David Viramontes

item.title: Using emojis in matplotlib
item.description: Last month, as I was writing the blog post with all the statistics for the blog's two year anniversary, the "Blog statistics after two years" post, I decided that I really wanted to have a plot with emojis. From the moment I thought of this I knew it couldn't simply …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: Blog statistics after two years
item.description:
Wow, it's been two years since I've started this blog! I'm really happy that I've still managed to keep up the one post per month. Hopefully I'll be able to keep it up for the third one!

For this post I thought it would be fun to look through some …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: Discovering the comfort of loudspeakers
item.description:
In 2014, while on a school trip to Germany, I bought a Razer Tiamat headset. The sound was great and for the most part it was comfortable. Its only issue was the clamping force, which was a bit too much, and gave me headaches after long hours of use.

I …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: Learning from SerenityOS
item.description: One day I was scrolling through Reddit as usual, when I saw a post linking to this blog post: I quit my job to focus on SerenityOS full time. I was intrigued by the backstory, the premise of this OS, and also by the fact that its development was being …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: Running LineageOS on my Nexus 5X
item.description:
This month marks one year since I bought a Nexus 5X and started using LineageOS, so I thought I'd share my experience.

Setting up

About one year ago, a friend told me he found a really good deal for a Nexus 5X online. The Nexus 5X is an old phone …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: Learning x86-64 assembly basics
item.description:
Recently I decided to learn assembly. I already had a reasonable understanding of how it worked due to some classes that touched the subject in university, however I never had the opportunity to really write assembly code.

Since my everyday computer is an x86-64 machine, it made most sense to …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: Making internal linking in pelican effortless
item.description: One interesting feature of Pelican, the static site generator I use for this blog, is the internal link expansion syntax with {}. It is documented here. Some examples are {filename}, {static} and {author}. The purpose of the syntax is to have shorter and easier aliases to link to internal content in …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: The menus in my system
item.description:
Another post going through stuff I set up for my desktop environment a couple years ago and that have used ever since 🙂. This time I'll show the menus I've created using rofi.

So, what is rofi? It's basically a program where you feed a list of options to it, and …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: The blocks in my status bar
item.description: Five years ago when I moved to the i3 window manager, I started using its status bar, the i3bar. It is text based, and it's up to you what gets shown there. However it is not very modular: it's weird to combine different information to be shown since everything has …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: Resurrecting a computer on the go
item.description:
Earlier this month I was spending a few weeks in another country. It was late at night and I was once again looking over my personal files and thinking if there was a better way to organize them in folders.

After thinking for a bit I decided on a new …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: My journey to a good backup system
item.description: I'm a bit of a data hoarder. I still have some of the first programs I've ever written, photos I've taken on trips and drawings I did many years ago, to name a few. And since I don't trust some company to store all of this data for me, it …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: Owning my Kindle
item.description: Several years ago my mother gave me a Kindle Paperwhite 2. I have read a few books on it since, but it never felt like it was really mine. Locking the screen showed some annoying ad, all books bought from Amazon were protected by DRM, books transferred through USB only …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: Blog customizations
item.description:
I've been using pelican as my static blog generator since I started this blog about one year ago. It only took a bit of configuration to get the blog up and running, and a bit of searching through pelicanthemes to find nikhil-theme which is the theme I'm still using.

While …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: One year of blog
item.description:
It's been a full year since I started this blog! So I thought I'd take this chance to talk a bit about the blog itself: How it started and my thoughts on it.

Origins

I had already thought a bit about having my own blog. Having a little corner of …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: gRPC: The front door for Node.js developers
item.description: Helpful resources and notes from learning gRPC as a Node.js developer, with a focus on practical next steps.
item.author: Ash Ryan Arnwine

item.title: Keeping track of my packages
item.description: As I previously mentioned in the "Moving to Wayland" post, I recently moved to a new computer. Moving can be very annoying if you use a heavily configured system and don't have all the configurations easily available to just move over. Since I do regular backups of my files, which …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: Learning music theory by writing melodies
item.description:
Hey, first non-technical blog post 🙂.

Anyway, I haven't talked about this before here, but I'm really interested in playing the piano. When I was younger I took some guitar classes, but I always wanted to play the piano, and a couple years ago I finally got one! I've been playing …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: Moving to Wayland
item.description: In the middle of January, my computer decided to surprise me, and not in a good way. Differently from all the quirks I've come to expect from it after all these 6 years of use — faulty keyboard, flashing screen, bad audio jack — this time it was worse, and it wasn't …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: Ledsticker: My first holistic SW + HW project
item.description: It was in the middle of 2019. I was taking classes in Embedded Systems Laboratory and had to make the final project of my choice. I had the idea to do some sort of a Guitar Hero using a keyboard for the input, an 8x8 LED matrix to display the …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: Organization beyond Taskwarrior
item.description:
In the previous article of this series, I went into all my Taskwarrior and VIT customizations, and my workflow with them, that enables me to organize my tasks and get them done. Tasks, however, aren't the whole story when getting organized.

Another crucial component of organization is having a calendar …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: Managing my tasks using VIT
item.description:
Two years ago I decided to get more organized about my life. During that time I read the Getting Things Done book and discovered Taskwarrior, a task manager for the terminal which doesn't get in the way.

I greatly appreciated Taskwarrior's simplicity and customizability, but after some time, the need …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: Astoria Tech Meetup #22: Containerizing a Node.js app... LIVE!
item.description: Our next virtual event is this week. We’ll be containerizing the Astoria Digital MuckRock project.
item.author: Ash Ryan Arnwine

item.title: Number sorting gotcha with JavaScript
item.description: Problem: Sorted arrays are coming back in an unexpected order... sometimes. Safari is behaving differently than Chrome and Node.js. Let’s look at what to do.
item.author: Ash Ryan Arnwine

item.title: Porting a flash LED driver upstream
item.description:
Now that I had a working serial cable for my Nexus 5, as described in the "Making an UART cable for the Nexus 5" post, I was ready to face some action and help in upstreaming.

Looking through Brian Masney's TODO page there were a couple options, but the one …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: I'd Rather Be Scripting, episode 1
item.description: Kerri Shotts and I started a little podcast called I'd Rather Be Scripting.
item.author: Ash Ryan Arnwine

item.title: Bulk file editing with ranger
item.description: My file manager of choice is ranger. It's terminal-based, provides keybind mapping for everything making me more efficient in navigating my files, and it's incredibly extensible by enabling the creation of custom commands in python. If that wasn't enough, it also has a ton of other great features (extensible file …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: Playlist generation with MPD
item.description: Music is life. I really love listening to music, although not the same kind of music all the time. Most of the time though, anything goes: I like to listen to any of the songs I have at random. But when I'm doing something that needs concentrating (like writing this …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: Automatic context detection for Taskwarrior
item.description: One of the main ideas of GTD is to have a context associated with each task, so that it is very easy to see which tasks can be done in your current context. I organize my tasks with Taskwarrior, so to make it work with contexts, when adding a new …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: Astoria Tech Meetup #19: Streamlining with Docker Compose
item.description: A virtual event coming up on Wednesday, August 26 from 6:30pm to 8:00pm
item.author: Ash Ryan Arnwine

item.title: Setting up mbsync to work with XOAUTH2
item.description: For a long time I used offlineimap to synchronize my emails between the email providers and my computer. Having access to all my emails offline on my computer is pretty handy. But after seeing the brutal efficiency advantage of mbsync over offlineimap, and having had delay issues with offlineimap myself …

item.title: Spin up a Node.js/Express server and a Postgres database
item.description: Quick steps to create a Node.js, Express, and Postgres playground.

item.title: MuckRock API
item.description: Looking at how to analyze basic health metrics of the FOIA request system using the MuckRock API.
item.author: Ash Ryan Arnwine

item.title: Fuzzy search with Fuse.js
item.description: Helping users around spelling errors with fuss-free JavaScript library for fuzzy search.

item.title: Astoria Digital on Computerworld
item.description: Our open source Volunteer Dispatch bot is available for use by other organizations.
item.author: Ash Ryan Arnwine

item.title: Finding and killing Node.js processes on Mac
item.description: How to find the process using a busy local port on macOS and kill it so your Node.js dev server can restart.
item.author: Ash Ryan Arnwine

item.title: First steps in Rust
item.description: Getting started with Rust.
item.author: Ash Ryan Arnwine

item.title: Making an UART cable for the Nexus 5
item.description: Recently, me and a friend started digging into making the Nexus 5 run the mainline Linux kernel. The purpose of this is, apart from a great learning experience, to make the Nexus 5 run a Linux distro, like PostmarketOS, instead of Android, while also having lifetime updates delivered from the …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: Taking an in-person developer event online
item.description: Is online-only the future of events? The good and the difficult in taking a developer event online.
item.author: Ash Ryan Arnwine

item.title: Make a micro repo
item.description: How splitting off into a micro repo can speed up your iterations and help you produce better results.

item.title: Cloning Dates in Moment.js
item.description: Avoiding unexpected date mutations by cloning.

item.title: CLI module for Node.js
item.description: My go-to node modules for basic command line argument parsing.

item.title: Share edit in Lightroom
item.description: Another new thing that came to Lightroom yesterday is the Share Edit (beta) feature.

item.title: The Lightroom-Photoshop round trip on iPad is here
item.description: The cross-app workflow sings, and I'm excited to have Photoshop available to me as a companion to Lightroom on iPad.

item.title: Shelves CLI
item.description: So now I've got this little Node.js shelves CLI that I likely will never need again.
item.author: Ash Ryan Arnwine

item.title: Creating movie and game lists using Taskwarrior
item.description: I like watching movies and playing games, but I also like to keep track of which movies I've watched and which games I've beaten. I also got into the habit of rating each movie I watch. For the longest time, though, I have kept track of these collections informally in …
item.author: Nicolas F. R. A. Prado

item.title: Node module for changing your MAC address
item.description: Spoof is a Node.js module that makes it super simple to change your MAC address on the fly. For macOS, Windows, and Linux.
item.author: Ash Ryan Arnwine

item.title: Playball CLI
item.description: Check out the Playball CLI for watching MLB games in your terminal.

item.title: Upstate bird
item.description: A bird photographed on a trail hike in Garrison, New York, with a Fujifilm X-T20 and XF 50-140mm lens.

item.description: A pull request for bootstrapping XD plugins with the xdpm CLI.

item.title: “Moving to zsh” talk at MacSysAdmin 2019
item.description: Check out the recording of “Moving to zsh” from MacSysAdmin 2019 if you’re looking for a way in to learning about zsh, the new default shell in macOS.

item.title: Ghost CMS
item.description: I’m going with the Ghost CMS for publishing this blog.

item.title: XD plugin modals: Handle your exits
item.description: There are multiple ways for a user to exit a modal-based XD plugin, and for your plugin’s code to handle those exits. Let’s take a look.

item.description: Shelling into a local Mac with Prompt 2 for iOS by Panic, an ssh client for iPhone and iPad.

item.title: Finding out where zsh aliases and settings are defined
item.description: Grepping my zsh setup shows that many of my shell aliases and settings are coming from Oh My Zsh.

item.description: A few basic points to keep in mind when creating modal dialog UI for XD plugins.

item.title: Learning about your zsh aliases
item.description: Notes from learning how zsh aliases work, including where they are defined and how Oh My Zsh changes the picture.

item.title: Brushing up on zsh and Oh My Zsh
item.description: Since macOS has now moved from bash to zsh, it seems like a fine time to revisit the basics of my preferred shell.

item.title: Restarting
item.description: It's the Saturday after Adobe MAX, coming down from what is a very busy time each year, so I wanted to start somewhere simple: my Terminal.
item.author: Ash Ryan Arnwine
